# ADR-004: Auto-Generated CLI and API Reference

## Status

Accepted

## Date

2026-04-15

## Context

AGH has 60+ CLI commands (14 command groups) and 80+ HTTP API endpoints. Maintaining hand-written reference documentation for this surface area is error-prone and will lag behind code changes. The CLI is built with Cobra (Go) and the HTTP API uses Gin.

## Decision

Auto-generate reference documentation:

- **CLI**: Use Cobra's built-in `doc.GenMarkdownTree()` to generate markdown, then post-process into MDX with Fumadocs frontmatter. Triggered via `make cli-docs`.
- **API**: Export an OpenAPI specification from the Gin router, then use Fumadocs' `fumadocs-openapi` integration to render interactive API docs. Triggered via `make api-spec`.

## Alternatives Considered

### Alternative 1: Custom JSON export + TypeScript MDX generator

- **Description**: Add `agh doc --format json` to export command tree as structured JSON. A TypeScript script transforms JSON into MDX.
- **Pros**: Full control over MDX output format, custom components, Fumadocs-specific metadata.
- **Cons**: Must build and maintain both the Go JSON exporter and the TS transformer. More code for the same result.
- **Why rejected**: Cobra's GenMarkdownTree is well-tested and produces adequate output. Post-processing markdown to MDX is simpler than building a custom pipeline.

### Alternative 2: Hand-written reference with staleness detection

- **Description**: Write all reference docs manually. A CI script diffs against cobra output to detect stale pages.
- **Pros**: Maximum prose quality. Each command gets curated examples and context.
- **Cons**: 60+ commands to write and maintain. Staleness detection only flags problems, doesn't fix them.
- **Why rejected**: Doesn't scale with AGH's command surface. Auto-generation covers 90% of the value; manual prose can be added as MDX annotations alongside generated content.

## Consequences

### Positive

- Reference docs stay in sync with code automatically
- `make cli-docs` and `make api-spec` can be CI gates — docs must regenerate cleanly
- Fumadocs OpenAPI integration provides interactive try-it-out for API endpoints
- Reduces documentation maintenance burden by ~25 pages of reference content

### Negative

- Generated CLI docs may lack curated examples — need a mechanism to inject custom prose alongside generated content
- OpenAPI export requires adding route metadata/annotations to the Gin handlers
- Build dependency: site build depends on Go binary being compilable

### Risks

- Cobra GenMarkdownTree output format may not match Fumadocs frontmatter expectations. Mitigate with a post-processing script.
- OpenAPI export from Gin is not built-in — requires a library (e.g., swaggo/swag or manual spec). Mitigate by evaluating libraries during implementation.

## Implementation Notes

- Add `doc` subcommand to `cmd/agh/` that calls `doc.GenMarkdownTree(rootCmd, outputDir)`
- Post-processing script: strip cobra boilerplate, add Fumadocs frontmatter (`title`, `description`), convert to MDX
- For API: evaluate `swaggo/swag` vs hand-maintained `openapi.json` — start with hand-maintained for alpha, migrate to auto-gen later
- Makefile targets: `cli-docs`, `api-spec`, `site-codegen` (runs both)

## References

- https://github.com/spf13/cobra/blob/main/doc/md_docs.md
- https://fumadocs.dev/docs/ui/openapi
